figures.caption: Figure 3. Visual demonstration of domain misalignment. Foundation models without LoRA adaptation hallucinate natural image features (petri dish, waffle) into CT reconstructions due to metal streak artifacts.

Figure 2. Visual comparison of artifact reduction methods. (a) Noisy inputs, (b) ADN, (c) Rise-MAR, (d) OSCNet+ and (e) Our reconstructions. The bottom row shows the absolute error maps relative to the ground truth. Note how our method significantly reduces streak artifacts while preserving anatomical structure.

Table 1. Quantitative comparison on the 1,000-sample CT-MAR test set. Bold: best among trained models. Underline: second best among trained models. VAE ceiling is not a trained MAR model — it encodes and decodes clean GT images without any diffusion, representing the theoretical upper bound imposed by the VAE bottleneck.
